summary: Draft PR adding a large partial decompilation of the data-delete menu module `src/melee/mn/mndatadel.c`, plus more specific `mndatadel` static/header types. It was closed without merge and had no human review text in the slice. The decomp.dev bot reported +672 matched code bytes, +48 matched data bytes, 3 new matches, and 11 unmatched-function improvements, with one small `.sdata2` regression.
what_changed: Replaced many `/// #...` stubs in `mndatadel.c` with C implementations for data-delete menu behavior, including `mnDataDel_8024E940`, `mnDataDel_8024EA6C`, `mnDataDel_8024EBC8`, `fn_8024F1D4`, `fn_8024F318`, `fn_8024F840`, `fn_8024FBA4`, `fn_8024FC48`, `mnDataDel_8024FE4C`, and `mnDataDel_80250170`.

Added local declarations for several `gm_1601` functions with signatures used by the new code: `gm_801647D0`, `gm_801641CC`, `gm_80164430`, `gm_80164250`, `gm_801641E4`, `gm_801603B0`, and `gm_8016505C`.

Added includes needed by the new menu logic, including `baselib/gobjuserdata.h`, `baselib/memory.h`, `baselib/sislib.h`, `gm/gm_16F1.h`, `gm/gm_1A3F.h`, `gm/gmmain_lib.h`, `lb/lbarchive.h`, `lb/lbaudio_ax.h`, `lb/lbcardgame.h`, and `ty/toy.h`.

Introduced `struct MnDataDelSelectData` and `struct MnDataDelUserData` in `mndatadel.static.h`, with fields for selected item, confirmation state, deleted-item flags, label text, and cached menu joints.
Refined `struct WarnCmnData` comments and changed `struct MnDataDelData` scalar sequences into arrays: `x3C[7]` for menu item joint indices and `x58[6]` for SIS label IDs.

Added static inline helper `SetItemChildAnim` to preserve a child JObj's current frame, request enabled/disabled animation frames, apply `mn_8022F3D8` with material/object masks, and restore the saved frame.

Updated public prototypes in `mndatadel.h`, replacing `UNK_PARAMS` for `mnDataDel_8024EBC8` with `HSD_JObj* root, u8 arg1, u8 arg2, u8 arg3`, and giving several callbacks explicit `HSD_GObj*` parameter names.
Added comments to `MenuInputState` fields in `mnmain.h`, describing `cooldown` as an input cooldown timer and noting `x2`/`x4` reset behavior as TODOs.

Implemented archive/model setup in `mnDataDel_80250170`, including loading sections from `mn_804D6BB8`, setting menu state `cur_menu = 0x18`, initializing cooldown, creating menu objects, and installing `fn_8024F840` as a process.

Implemented user-data allocation and initialization in `mnDataDel_8024FE4C`, including `HSD_MemAlloc(0x30)`, an assert on failure, `GObj_InitUserData` with `HSD_Free`, cached JObj lookup, item model creation, item animation setup, and SIS label creation.
smart_moves: Converted repeated per-item joint and label fields into arrays, making later menu-selection code indexable instead of relying on many separate `x3C`, `x40`, `x44`, etc. fields.
Centralized a repeated item-child animation sequence in `SetItemChildAnim`, preserving the active animation frame before toggling the child state and then restoring it.
Used explicit `HSD_GObj*` and `HSD_JObj*` parameters in headers rather than opaque `UNK_PARAMS`, improving call-site documentation and future type propagation.

Preserved subtle matching details in the decompiled functions, such as padding locals, temporary aliases like `root_copy`, an unused `arg3` cast noted as affecting caller register allocation, and seemingly redundant casts/indexing where needed.
Kept menu UI state in a dedicated `MnDataDelUserData` allocation attached to the GObj, with fields for current selection, confirmation visibility, deleted-item flags, label text, and cached joints.

The code reuses existing menu helper functions for frame queries and animation loops (`mn_8022F298`, `mn_8022EFD8`, `mn_8022EE84`, `mn_8022F3D8`) rather than open-coding lower-level JObj animation handling.
decomp_lessons: For menu modules, reconstructing user-data structs can unlock many callbacks at once because process callbacks repeatedly access the same selected-item, cursor, text, and joint fields.
Arrays should be preferred when adjacent unknown fields are used with indexed access patterns; here `x3C[7]` and `x58[6]` made joint and SIS label access clearer and closer to the generated code.
When matching animation code, save the current JObj frame, request a temporary state frame, apply a mask with `mn_8022F3D8`, animate, then restore the saved frame and apply the runtime mask.

Button-handling callbacks often need exact cooldown and process handoff behavior: this PR repeatedly sets `mn_804D6BC8.cooldown`, kills the old process with `HSD_GObjProc_8038FE24`, installs a new one with `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54`, and copies `HSD_GObj_804D783C` into `proc->flags_3`.
SIS text lifetime matters in menu decomp: existing `HSD_Text*` values are freed with `HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4` before replacement and nulled when dialogs close.
Closed draft PRs can still contain useful partial matches, but future work should treat the exact code as provisional because it was not merged and had no human reviewer validation in the slice.
naming_conventions: Module symbols keep the project-address convention, e.g. `mnDataDel_8024E940`, `mnDataDel_80250170`, and callback-style `fn_8024F840`.
Unknown-but-structured fields remain offset-like (`x0`, `x2`, `x3C`, `x58`) while comments document observed roles such as selected item, dialog visibility, and SIS label IDs.
New local struct names follow module context: `MnDataDelSelectData`, `MnDataDelUserData`, and `MnDataDelData`.
Callback parameters are named by role when evidence supports it (`gobj`, `root`) and left generic where less clear (`arg0`, `arg1`, `arg2`, `arg3`).
Static asset/model descriptors retain address-style names such as `mnDataDel_804A0918`, `mnDataDel_804A0928`, and `mnDataDel_804A0938`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added explicit stack padding locals such as `_pad[8]`, `_pad[16]`, and `_pad[0x58]` to influence stack layout and matching.
Used temporary variables and aliases (`root_copy`, `const_zero`, `select_ptr`, `end_frame_ptr`, `data_tbl`, `data2`) to shape register allocation and load ordering.
Included a `(void) arg3` note in `mnDataDel_8024EBC8` because the unused parameter affects caller register allocation.
Used casts through raw pointers or array views, e.g. `((u8*) data)[offset]`, `((s32*) &menu_data->x3C)[i]`, and `((u16*) &menu_data->x58)[selected_item]`, to match known layout/access patterns.
Used XOR-with-zero style boolean checks in `fn_8024F840`, preserving compiler-like branch structure for button flags.
Kept local declarations for `gm_1601` functions in `mndatadel.c` to supply exact signatures before broader header cleanup.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The only feedback-like evidence was the decomp.dev bot report: 3 new matches (`mnDataDel_80250170`, `fn_8024F1D4`, `.bss`), 11 improvements in unmatched functions, and one `.sdata2` regression.
follow_up_queries: Find the later merged PR or commit that completed or superseded `pr/mn-datadel`.
Search for `MnDataDelUserData` or `SetItemChildAnim` in current master to see whether these struct/helper names survived review.
Check whether the local `gm_1601` declarations were later moved into proper `gm` headers.
Investigate the `.sdata2` regression reported by decomp.dev before reusing the exact float/static-data layout.
Compare current `mndatadel.c` against this draft for improved matches in `fn_8024F318`, `fn_8024F840`, and `mnDataDel_8024FE4C`.
